sourceTitle: A guide to connecting projects and meeting notes

questions.question: Which Notion property type lets you link one database to another?

options.explanation: Formula calculates values; it doesn't create links between databases.
Relation properties are specifically designed to connect entries across databases.
Rollup works on top of a Relation; it can’t create the initial link.
A plain text field can hold a URL, but it doesn’t maintain a live database relationship.

questions.question: Where can you browse the 10,000+ templates mentioned in the guide?

options.explanation: The guide points readers to the template gallery for thousands of templates.
Settings let you adjust workspace preferences, not browse templates.
The help center contains articles, not the template collection.
YouTube hosts videos, not the template library.

questions.question: What is the first step to connect the Projects database with the Tasks database?

options.text: Delete all existing properties in the Projects database
options.explanation: Removing properties isn’t required and would delete useful data.

options.text: Add a new property and select the Relation type pointing to the Tasks database
options.explanation: The guide instructs you to add a new Relation property to link the two databases.

options.text: Export both databases to CSV and merge them externally
options.explanation: Exporting to CSV breaks the live connection that Notion provides.

options.text: Change the view of the Projects database to a board view
options.explanation: Changing a view doesn’t create a relationship between databases.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a benefit of connecting databases in Notion?

options.text: Team members can quickly find all relevant pages
options.explanation: The guide explicitly mentions faster discovery of relevant pages.
Providing quick access to linked documents is a stated advantage.

options.text: Automatic email notifications for linked items
options.explanation: The article never mentions email notifications as a benefit.

options.text: Projects, tasks, and meeting notes stay connected
options.explanation: Keeping related items linked is a core purpose of the guide.

questions.question: If an ad blocker stops the video from playing, what does the guide suggest you do?

options.explanation: The guide doesn’t ask you to change your ad‑blocking settings.
A direct link to the YouTube version is provided as an alternative.
Skipping would prevent you from seeing the content the guide references.
Changing browsers isn’t mentioned as a solution in the article.

questions.question: After creating a Relation from Projects to Tasks, you want each Task to automatically show its related Meeting Notes. Which Notion property should you add to the Tasks database?

options.explanation: Formulas compute based on fields but can’t pull data from another database without a Relation.
Rollups retrieve data from a linked Relation, allowing Tasks to display related Meeting Notes.
Multi‑select stores static tags; it doesn’t fetch data from other databases.
Date fields record timestamps and cannot pull related record information.

questions.question: If a topic isn’t covered in this guide, where does the article direct you for more help?

options.explanation: The template gallery offers templates, not additional support articles.
YouTube provides videos, but the guide points to a specific help resource.
The guide explicitly suggests visiting the help center for unanswered questions.
A community forum isn’t mentioned in the article as a next step.
